---
title: "Contributing"
---

## Development setup

```bash
git clone git@github.com:bsdlab/xileh.git
cd xileh
uv sync --extra all
```

## Branching model

- `main` — stable, released code. Documentation and PyPI publishing trigger
  off this branch.
- `develop` — integration branch for ongoing work.
- Feature branches — `feature/<name>`, `fix/<name>`, etc., branched off
  `develop` (or `main` for hotfixes), merged back via pull request.

## Commit messages

Follow [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/):

- `feat:` a new feature
- `fix:` a bug fix
- `docs:` documentation only
- `test:` adding or fixing tests
- `refactor:` code change that neither fixes a bug nor adds a feature
- `chore:` tooling / packaging changes

## Running checks

```bash
# tests with coverage
uv run --extra dev pytest tests/ -v --cov=src/xileh

# lint
uv run --extra dev ruff check src/ tests/
```

## Code style

- NumPy-style docstrings for public functions and classes.
- Type hints on function signatures.
- Keep changes surgical; prefer established libraries over custom code.

## Releasing

1. Bump `version` in `pyproject.toml`.
2. Tag the commit: `git tag vX.Y.Z && git push origin vX.Y.Z`.
3. The `publish-pypi` workflow builds and publishes to PyPI via OIDC trusted
   publishing.
